title: Ilex walsinghamii
uses: Ilex walsinghamii, also known as the Walsingham's holly, is a plant species that is native to the mountainous regions of the Philippines. Unfortunately, information about its medicinal uses is scarce. Ilex walsinghamii is primarily a horticultural plant due to its ornamental value, but its potential medicinal properties have not been extensively studied or documented. More research is needed to fully understand any potential health benefits associated with this plant species. Traditional herbal remedies in the Philippines may utilize Ilex walsinghamii, but its specific applications and uses remain largely unverified.
taxonomy: Ilex walsinghamii is a species of holly native to the Philippines. It belongs to the family Aquifoliaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the holly family.
